cover: Split-screen comparison infographic: 'Agentforce 2.0: What Actually Changed from 1.0'. Deep navy blue bold header. LEFT COLUMN 'Agentforce 1.0' soft red/grey with blocks for 'single-agent handoff', 'prompt-heavy reasoning', 'limited orchestration visibility', and tangled arrows between Agent, Flow, Apex Action, Data Cloud. RIGHT COLUMN 'Agentforce 2.0 Winter 26' soft green/blue with blocks for 'multi-agent orchestration', 'Atlas Reasoning Engine v2', 'custom reasoning steps', 'native Data Cloud vector search'. Central glowing node 'Agentforce 2.0 Orchestrator' with clean straight lines to Sales Agent, Service Agent, Compliance Agent, Knowledge Agent. Bottom speedometer labeled 'Fewer brittle handoffs'. Clean vector SaaS aesthetic, deep navy/teal/blue palette on light blue-grey background.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "AGENTFORCE 2.0 MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "AGENTFORCE 2.0 MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "How Atlas Reasoning Engine v2 Routes Between Specialised Agents" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Atlas Routing" with a bot icon, "Focused Prompts" with a target icon, "Higher Confidence" with a filter icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "AGENTFORCE 1.0 — Single Overloaded Agent". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "AGENTFORCE 2.0 — Multi-Agent Orchestration". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Agentforce 1.0: one agent, huge prompt
// System prompt: 2,000 tokens of mixed policy
// "You handle warranty, billing, returns,
//  technical support, escalations, and routing"
// Result: wrong policy applied, low confidence,
// difficult to debug which rule was triggered
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    // Agentforce 2.0: specialised agents per topic
Topic: 'Warranty Claims'   → WarrantyAgent
Topic: 'Billing Disputes'  → BillingAgent
Topic: 'Technical Support' → TechAgent
// Atlas Reasoning Engine v2 classifies intent
// Routes to the RIGHT agent with FOCUSED prompt
// Each agent: narrow job, high confidence

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "One Agentforce Topic = one business domain, always", "Narrow system prompts → higher confidence scores", "Atlas Reasoning Engine v2 owns topic classification", "Custom reasoning steps let you inject deterministic logic", "Multi-agent handoff replaces brittle Flow-to-Flow chains".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Agentforce 2.0", "Atlas Reasoning Engine v2", "Winter 26", "Multi-Agent" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
